chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P.No.D-505 of 2024 C.P.No.D-1394 of 2025 C.P.No.D-1395 of 2025 Date Order with signature of Judge ________________________________________________________ 1. For order as to non-prosecution of Misc. No. 4671/2024 2. For order on office objection 3. For hearing of Misc. No. 2450/2024 4. For hearing of main case 21.10.2025 Malik Altaf Hussain advocate for the petitioners Ms. Saima Imdad Asstt. A.G Sindh -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Learned counsel for the petitioners would be satisfied, if these petitions are disposed of in terms of paragraph No.8 of order dated 03.09.2025 passed in C.P.No.D-1541 of 2025, which is reproduced below for the ease of reference: “8. At this point, we proposed remitting the case to the respondents’ competent authority for reconsideration. This would involve a thorough review of the service record of the petitioners, consideration of relevant Supreme Court judgments and granting the petitioners a proper hearing to reach a fresh decision within three months. As such, we find it appropriate to remit the matter to the Chief Secretary of Sindh. The Chief Secretary will coordinate with the Secretary of Education to convene a meeting. After examining the petitioners’ service records and promotion order, a new decision will be made within three months.” 2. Learned AAG has objected to such proposition. However, we find no merits in her objection as the same has been dealt in the order dated 03.09.2025. 3. Order accordingly in terms of above reproduced paragraph No.8. 4. Copy of this order shall be sent to Chief Secretary Sindh, who will coordinate with Secretary Education to convene a meeting and to examine the petitioners’ service records and promotion orders and pass a new decision in accordance with law within three months from the date of this order. 5.

Order accordingly in terms of above reproduced paragraph No.8. 4. Copy of this order shall be sent to Chief Secretary Sindh, who will coordinate with Secretary Education to convene a meeting and to examine the petitioners’ service records and promotion orders and pass a new decision in accordance with law within three months from the date of this order. 5. Petitions stand disposed of in the above terms along with listed/pending applications. 6. Office is directed to place copy of this order in the captioned connected petitions. HEAD OF CONST. BENCHES JUDGE Wasim PS
